layman_headline: Extreme fear grips the crypto market — patience is the best trade right now.
layman_explanation: The entire crypto market is in a deep slump with Bitcoin down over 6% this week and most altcoins bleeding even harder. The Fear & Greed index reads 18 — 'Extreme Fear' — meaning most investors are panicking. While extreme fear can eventually create great buying opportunities, right now there are no clear bottom signals in the charts. Jumping in too early during a panic can mean catching a falling knife.
layman_action: Stay in cash and watch from the sidelines. Wait for Bitcoin to stabilize and for fear to start easing before putting money to work. If NEAR drops to $1.80 with a clear bounce pattern, that could be the moment.
expert_reasoning: No candidate presents a high-conviction swing entry right now. VELVET is up 218% in 7 days with RSI at 70 — classic blow-off top risk with a risk/reward of just 0.66x, making it a hard pass. KITE and ENA have strongly bearish chart structures with Three Black Crows and Bearish Marubozu patterns. SKYAI dropped 31% with a doji showing indecision and below all MAs. WLD lacks any chart data. NEAR is the relative standout: it bounced 6.5% today, sits above its 20-day MA with neutral RSI at 54, and has a slightly bullish chart bias. However, BTC dominance at 55.8% is punishing alts, the Fear & Greed index at 18 is worsening (not yet bottoming), and the news cycle — capitulation risk with 50K BTC moved at a loss, gold/silver correlation dragging crypto — suggests more downside may come before a true reversal.
risk_factors: BTC could break below $60K triggering further altcoin liquidation cascades — NEAR would follow
Fear & Greed trend is still worsening (not bottoming), meaning capitulation may not be complete
High BTC dominance at 55.8% means capital is rotating out of alts — NEAR could underperform even if BTC stabilizes
watch_list: Bitcoin price action at the $58,000-$60,000 support zone — a breakdown triggers more alt pain
Fear & Greed index: watch for a turn from 'worsening' to 'improving' as the first bottoming signal
NEAR holding $1.80 support with increasing volume — would confirm accumulation and set up a buy
market_context: Bitcoin is clinging to $60K amid broad risk-off sentiment driven by gold/silver correlation breakdown and macro uncertainty. Extreme Fear at 18 with a worsening trend suggests the market hasn't found its floor yet — historically great long-term buying zones but premature for swing entries.
context_banner: Extreme Fear dominates crypto markets — discipline and patience outperform bravery until clear reversal signals emerge.

key_signals.description: Fear & Greed at 18 with worsening trend indicates capitulation phase is ongoing — historically great for long-term buys but too early for swing trades.
50K BTC moved at a loss signals fresh capitulation risk, meaning selling pressure hasn't exhausted yet.
Gold and silver selloff dragging Bitcoin down highlights macro correlation risk and broader risk-off environment.
NEAR RSI at 54 is neutral — not oversold enough for a high-conviction mean-reversion entry during a bear phase.
No candidates show volume-confirmed breakouts from support — all rallies are tentative and lack conviction.
